name: Maquiritari

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Ye'kwana (maqu1238).

notes: Skeleton. Cariban language of the Venezuela-Brazil border region, self-named Ye'kwana (also Maquiritari), in its Spanish-influenced practical orthography. ch=[tʃ], j=[h], ñ=[ɲ], and the back/central unrounded vowels ö=[ɤ] and ü=[ɨ] are mapped; d denotes an implosive/flap [ɗ]. Contrastive vowel nasalization and predictable stress/length are not recovered from spelling and are left unencoded, raising the PER floor.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
